name: JsonCodec
comment:
Build json decoders and encoders simultaneously, conserving the need for
functions that do each in simple cases.  This allows a single datum to embody
both the encoder and the decoder for a type, reducing some duplication and
redundancy.  In simple cases, a codec can be built exclusively by composing
other codecs.

In more complex cases, ```first``` could be used, which allows fields to be
encoded and extracted separately.

To just specify a decoder and encoder separately, use ```init```.

    module Session exposing (..)

    import JsonCodec exposing (Codec)
    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Json.Decode as JD
    import Json.Encode as JE

    type alias Session =
        { queue : List String
        , playing : Maybe (String, Float)
        , likeCategories : Dict String Int
        }

    type Alternative = A | Unknown String

    -- An example showing the use of ```map``` to augment a
    -- bijective encoding.
    altcoder : Codec Alternative
    altcoder =
        JsonCodec.string
        |> JsonCodec.map
            (\a -> if a == "A" then A else Unknown a)
            (\v ->
                case v of
                    A -> "A"
                    Unknown x -> x
            )

    type Picky = OptA | OptB
    pickyOpts : Dict String Picky
    pickyOpts = Dict.fromList [("A", OptA), ("B", OptB)]

    -- An example showing the use of ```andThen``` to exclude
    -- bad values.
    pickycoder : Codec Picky
    pickycoder =
        JsonCodec.string |> JsonCodec.andThen
            (((flip Dict.get) pickyOpts)
            >> Maybe.map JD.succeed
            >> Maybe.withDefault (JD.fail "Option must be A or B")
            )
            (\p -> pickyOpts
            |> Dict.toList
            |> List.filterMap (\(k,v) -> if v == p then Just k else Nothing)
            |> List.head
            |> Maybe.withDefault "A"
            )

    -- A codec that can stand on its own and also be reused.
    playingSerializer : Codec (Maybe (String,Float))
    playingSerializer =
        JsonCodec.nullable
            (JsonCodec.object2
                (,)
                identity
                ("u", JsonCodec.string)
                ("p", JsonCodec.float)
            )

    -- Simple codec built with composition.
    serializer : Codec Session
    serializer =
        JsonCodec.object3
            Session
            (\s -> (s.queue,s.playing,s.likeCategories))
            ("queue", JsonCodec.list JsonCodec.string)
            ("playing", playingSerializer)
            ("like", JsonCodec.dict JsonCodec.int)

    -- Codec built in application style
    type alias Test = { i : Int, b : Bool, f : Float, s : String }

    codec = 
        Test
        |> JC.first "i" JC.int .i
        |> JC.next "b" JC.bool .b
        |> JC.next "f" JC.float .f
        |> JC.next "s" JC.string .s
        |> JC.end

    x = JD.decodeString (JC.decoder be) "{\"i\":3,\"b\":false,\"f\":3.14,\"s\":\"hi there\"}"
    -- Ok { i = 3, b = False, f = 3.14, s = "hi there" }

# Type
@docs Codec, Builder

# Simple codecs
@docs string, bool, int, float, nullable, list, array, dict, keyValuePairs, singleton, object2, object3, object4, object5, object6, object7, object8, null, succeed, fail

# Transform in both directions
@docs map, andThen

# Decoding alternatives
@docs oneOf

# Construction
@docs decoder, encoder, init

# Application
@docs first, next, end

unions.name: Builder
unions.comment: Type of a codec builder used with ```first```, ```next``` and ```end```

aliases.name: Codec
aliases.comment:
The type of codecs constructed by the library.

You can extract a Json.Decode.Decoder with ```decoder``` and a function that
constructs ```Json.Encoder.Value``` with ```encoder```.

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Like map, but the decode function returns a decoder that will be evaluated
next, rather than just mapping the value.

    -- An example showing the use of ```andThen``` to exclude
    -- bad values.
    pickycoder : Codec Picky
    pickycoder =
        JsonCodec.string |> JsonCodec.andThen
            (((flip Dict.get) pickyOpts)
            >> Maybe.map JD.succeed
            >> Maybe.withDefault (JD.fail "Option must be A or B")
            )
            (\p -> pickyOpts
            |> Dict.toList
            |> List.filterMap (\(k,v) -> if v == p then Just k else Nothing)
            |> List.head
            |> Maybe.withDefault "A"
            )
values.type: (a -> Decoder b) -> (b -> a) -> Codec a -> Codec b

values.name: array
values.comment: Codec that produces and consumes arrays.

values.name: bool
values.comment: Codec matching and producing bools.

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Get a Json.Decode.Decoder from a codec.

values.name: dict
values.comment: Codec that produces and consumes dictionaries of other values.

values.name: encoder
values.comment: Get a function that encodes Json.Encode.Value from a codec.

values.name: end
values.comment: Make the final step to turn a result from Builder into Codec.
values.type: Builder (Decoder o) (o -> List ( String, Value )) -> Codec o

values.name: fail
values.comment:
Codec that produces a constant encoded value but always fails decoding.
One might use this while prefiltering inputs based on their structure but ensure
that encoded json has the right structure.

values.name: first
values.comment:
Start composing a codec to decode a record using a series of function
applications.

Using:

- A field name
- A function that decodes the value ```v``` in that field
- A function that extracts the field value ```v``` from the finished record
- A record building function such as a type alias constructor 
(we'll call this ```o```)

Return:

- A partially constructed decoder, which given the rest of the parameters 
for ```o```, yields a ```Codec o```.

You can build record codecs of arbitrarily many parameters with this, the same
way other codecs are built, together using the same code.

An example:

    type alias X = { i : Int, s : String, b : Bool, f : Float }
    c =  JC.first "i" JC.int .i X 
      |> JC.next "s" JC.string .s
      |> JC.next "b" JC.bool .b
      |> JC.next "f" JC.float .f
      |> JC.end

    > JD.decodeString (JC.decoder c) "{\"i\":3,\"s\":\"hi\",\"b\":false,\"f\":1.9}"
    Ok { i = 3, s = "hi", b = False, f = 1.9 } : Result.Result String Repl.X
values.type: String -> Codec v -> (o -> v) -> (v -> b) -> Builder (Decoder b) (o -> List ( String, Value ))

values.name: float
values.comment: Codec matching and producing floats.

values.name: init
values.comment: Construct an arbitrary codec from a decoder and an encoder function.

values.name: int
values.comment: Codec matching and producing ints.

values.name: keyValuePairs
values.comment: Codec that produces and consumes key value pair lists of other values.

values.name: list
values.comment: Codec that produces and consumes lists.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Wrap the codec in a transformer that produces and consumes another type.

    type Alternative = A | Unknown String

    altcoder : Codec Alternative
    altcoder =
        Codec.string
        |> Codec.map
            (\a -> if a == "A" then A else Unknown a)
            (\v ->
                case v of
                    A -> "A"
                    Unknown x -> x
            )
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Codec a -> Codec b

values.name: next
values.comment:
Continue a partial codec from first, satisfying one more parameter of the
constructor function.
values.type: String -> Codec v -> (o -> v) -> Builder (Decoder (v -> b)) (o -> List ( String, Value )) -> Builder (Decoder b) (o -> List ( String, Value ))

values.name: null
values.comment: Codec that matches null, produces null.

values.name: nullable
values.comment: Codec that maps null to Nothing and vice versa.

values.name: object2
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 2 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> Codec x

values.name: object3
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 3 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> Codec x

values.name: object4
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 4 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c, d )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> ( String, Codec d ) -> Codec x

values.name: object5
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 5 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c, d, e )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> ( String, Codec d ) -> ( String, Codec e ) -> Codec x

values.name: object6
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 6 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c, d, e, f )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> ( String, Codec d ) -> ( String, Codec e ) -> ( String, Codec f ) -> Codec x

values.name: object7
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 7 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c, d, e, f, g )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> ( String, Codec d ) -> ( String, Codec e ) -> ( String, Codec f ) -> ( String, Codec g ) -> Codec x

values.name: object8
values.comment: Codec that matches and produces objects with 8 given named fields.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g -> h -> x) -> (x -> ( a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h )) -> ( String, Codec a ) -> ( String, Codec b ) -> ( String, Codec c ) -> ( String, Codec d ) -> ( String, Codec e ) -> ( String, Codec f ) -> ( String, Codec g ) -> ( String, Codec h ) -> Codec x

values.name: oneOf
values.comment: Match one of many decoders, encode using the given function.

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
Codec that matches a single field similar to Json.Decode and produces a
singleton object with 1 field.

values.name: string
values.comment: Codec matching and producing strings.

values.name: succeed
values.comment:
Codec that produces a constant decoded value and encodes to a constant value.
One might use this to check a field with a constant value, such as a version
number.
